edges.mechanism: SL binding to D14 (not hydrolysis) is required for D14 protein degradation; polyubiquitination and 26S proteasomal degradation, plus an alternative MAX2-independent pathway

evidence.title: Novel mechanisms of strigolactone-induced DWARF14 degradation in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Sanchez Martin-Fontecha E, Cardinale F, Burger M, Prandi C, Cubas P
evidence.evidence_sentence: SL binding but not hydrolysis is required for D14 degradation
evidence.claim: SL binding triggers conformational change driving D14 degradation

edges.mechanism: SCFMAX2 partially mediates SL-induced D14 ubiquitination; in max2 mutants D14 is destabilized but less efficiently than in WT, indicating an alternative MAX2-independent degradation pathway exists

evidence.title: Novel mechanisms of strigolactone-induced DWARF14 degradation in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Sanchez Martin-Fontecha E, Cardinale F, Burger M, Prandi C, Cubas P
evidence.evidence_sentence: in max2 mutants, D14 becomes destabilized although less efficiently so than in the wild type
evidence.claim: MAX2 partially required for D14 degradation; alternative pathway exists

edges.mechanism: SMXL6/7/8 binding to SL-D14 may mask D14 from the alternative (MAX2-independent) proteolytic machinery, slightly stabilizing D14 in the absence of MAX2

evidence.title: Novel mechanisms of strigolactone-induced DWARF14 degradation in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Sanchez Martin-Fontecha E, Cardinale F, Burger M, Prandi C, Cubas P
evidence.evidence_sentence: the interaction of the SMXLs with SL-D14 may interfere with the MAX2-independent pathway of D14 degradation, perhaps by making D14 less accessible
evidence.claim: SMXL678 partially shields D14 from non-MAX2 proteolysis

edges.mechanism: SL-D14 forms complex with SCFMAX2 and SMXL6/7/8; SMXLs are polyubiquitinated and degraded by the proteasome

evidence.title: Novel mechanisms of strigolactone-induced DWARF14 degradation in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Sanchez Martin-Fontecha E, Cardinale F, Burger M, Prandi C, Cubas P
evidence.evidence_sentence: SL signalling requires the formation of a complex between SL-D14, the E3-ubiquitin ligase complex SCFMAX2 and the transcriptional corepressors SMXL6/7/8
evidence.claim: SL-D14-MAX2 complex targets SMXL678 for degradation

edges.mechanism: SMXL7:LUC was destabilized by GR24-5DS in a dose-dependent manner at much lower SL concentrations than D14 - SMXL7 degradation is the more sensitive step in the pathway

evidence.title: Novel mechanisms of strigolactone-induced DWARF14 degradation in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Sanchez Martin-Fontecha E, Cardinale F, Burger M, Prandi C, Cubas P
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMXL7:LUC was destabilized by GR245DS in a dose-dependent manner at much lower SL concentrations than D14
evidence.claim: SL drives SMXL7 degradation more efficiently than D14 degradation

edges.mechanism: SMXL5 forms heteromeric complexes with SMXL7 and interacts with AtD14, attenuating SL signaling by interfering with AtD14-SMXL7 interactions and reducing SMXL7 degradation

evidence.title: SMXL5 attenuates strigolactone signaling in Arabidopsis thaliana by inhibiting SMXL7 degradation
evidence.authors: Li Q, Yu H, Chang W, Chang S, Guzman M, Faure L, Wallner ES, Yan H, Greb T, Wang L, Yao R, Nelson DC
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMXL5 attenuates strigolactone signaling by interfering with AtD14-SMXL7 interactions
evidence.claim: SMXL5 stabilizes SMXL7 by competing with D14

edges.mechanism: SMXL5 directly binds AtD14 and SMXL7, providing two possible competitive routes to inhibit SMXL7 degradation

evidence.title: SMXL5 attenuates strigolactone signaling in Arabidopsis thaliana by inhibiting SMXL7 degradation
evidence.authors: Li Q, Yu H, Chang W, Chang S, Guzman M, Faure L, Wallner ES, Yan H, Greb T, Wang L, Yao R, Nelson DC
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMXL5 interacts with AtD14 and SMXL7, providing two possible ways to inhibit SMXL7 degradation
evidence.claim: SMXL5 physically interacts with both D14 and SMXL7

edges.mechanism: Unlike SMXL6/7/8 (which are degraded by SCFMAX2), SMXL5 is resistant to MAX2-mediated degradation, allowing it to act as a stable buffer/competitor

evidence.title: SMXL5 attenuates strigolactone signaling in Arabidopsis thaliana by inhibiting SMXL7 degradation
evidence.authors: Li Q, Yu H, Chang W, Chang S, Guzman M, Faure L, Wallner ES, Yan H, Greb T, Wang L, Yao R, Nelson DC
evidence.evidence_sentence: are resistant to MAX2-mediated degradation
evidence.claim: SMXL5 is not degraded by SCFMAX2, unlike SMXL6/7/8

edges.mechanism: rac-GR24 treatment requires the SWI/SNF ATPase SPLAYED (SYD) for both BRC1 induction and the >1400 differentially accessible chromatin regions detected by ATAC-seq; SYD-dependent chromatin remodeling precedes transcriptional changes

evidence.title: Strigolactone induces D14-dependent large-scale changes in gene expression requiring SWI/SNF chromatin remodellers
evidence.authors: Humphreys JL, Beveridge CA, Tanurdzic M
evidence.evidence_sentence: this early and extensive modification of the chromatin landscape also requires SYD
evidence.claim: SYD is required for SL-induced chromatin remodeling and BRC1 induction

edges.mechanism: SPLAYED (SYD), a SWI/SNF chromatin-remodeling ATPase, is required for SL-mediated transcriptional induction of BRC1

evidence.title: Strigolactone induces D14-dependent large-scale changes in gene expression requiring SWI/SNF chromatin remodellers
evidence.authors: Humphreys JL, Beveridge CA, Tanurdzic M
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRANCHED1 (BRC1): Identified as a critical SL-induced transcription factor whose induction requires the SWI/SNF ATPase SPLAYED (SYD)
evidence.claim: SYD is necessary for SL-induced BRC1 expression

edges.mechanism: BRAHMA (BRM), a SWI/SNF ATPase paralog of SYD, is upregulated downstream of BRC1 induction

evidence.title: Strigolactone induces D14-dependent large-scale changes in gene expression requiring SWI/SNF chromatin remodellers
evidence.authors: Humphreys JL, Beveridge CA, Tanurdzic M
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRAHMA: A SWI/SNF ATPase homologue that is upregulated downstream of BRC1 induction
evidence.claim: BRM is induced downstream of SL/BRC1

edges.mechanism: Sucrose antagonizes SL-induced degradation of D53 (the rice SMXL678 ortholog), stabilizing D53 protein and promoting tillering even in the presence of SL

evidence.title: Sucrose promotes D53 accumulation and tillering in rice
evidence.authors: Patil SB, Barbier FF, Zhao J, Zafar SA, Uzair M, Sun Y, Fang J, Perez-Garcia MD, Bertheloot J, Sakr S, Fichtner F, Chabikwa TG, Yuan S, Beveridge CA, Li X
evidence.evidence_sentence: sucrose antagonizes the suppressive action of SL on tillering in rice and on the degradation of D53
evidence.claim: Sucrose stabilizes D53 against SL-induced degradation

edges.mechanism: Sucrose prevents SL-induced D14 degradation, blocking the upstream step of SL signaling

evidence.title: Sucrose promotes D53 accumulation and tillering in rice

evidence.evidence_sentence: Sucrose prevents SL-induced degradation of D14, the SL receptor involved in D53 degradation
evidence.claim: Sucrose stabilizes D14 against SL-induced degradation

edges.mechanism: Sucrose represses transcript abundance of D3 (the rice ortholog of Arabidopsis MAX2), inhibiting SCF complex availability for SL signaling

evidence.title: Sucrose promotes D53 accumulation and tillering in rice
evidence.authors: Patil SB, Barbier FF, Zhao J, Zafar SA, Uzair M, Sun Y, Fang J, Perez-Garcia MD, Bertheloot J, Sakr S, Fichtner F, Chabikwa TG, Yuan S, Beveridge CA, Li X
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sucrose inhibits the gene expression of D3, the orthologue of the Arabidopsis F-box MAX2 required for SL signalling
evidence.claim: Sucrose represses MAX2/D3 expression

edges.mechanism: Sucrose promotes tillering/branching by interfering with SL signaling at multiple steps (D14 stabilization, MAX2 repression, D53 stabilization)

evidence.title: Sucrose promotes D53 accumulation and tillering in rice
evidence.authors: Patil SB, Barbier FF, Zhao J, Zafar SA, Uzair M, Sun Y, Fang J, Perez-Garcia MD, Bertheloot J, Sakr S, Fichtner F, Chabikwa TG, Yuan S, Beveridge CA, Li X
evidence.evidence_sentence: sucrose inhibits SL response by affecting key components of SL signalling
evidence.claim: Sucrose promotes branching by antagonizing SL signaling

edges.mechanism: SMXL7/SMXL678 directly represses BRC1/TCP18 transcript abundance; chimeric SMXL with SMXL7 N-terminus reduced BRC1 transcripts similarly to SMXL7

evidence.title: An N-terminal domain specifies developmental control by the SMAX1-LIKE family of transcriptional regulators in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Chang SH, George W, Nelson DC

evidence.claim: SMXL7 N-domain is required and sufficient to repress BRC1 transcripts

edges.mechanism: SMXL7 (and SMAX1) physically interact with TCP18/BRC1 protein in yeast two-hybrid via the N-terminal domain - a direct PPI partnership in addition to transcriptional repression

evidence.title: An N-terminal domain specifies developmental control by the SMAX1-LIKE family of transcriptional regulators in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Chang SH, George W, Nelson DC
evidence.evidence_sentence: TCP18/BRC1, a key regulator of branching, putatively interacted with SMAX1 and SMXL7 through the N domain
evidence.claim: SMXL7 directly binds BRC1 protein via N-domain

edges.mechanism: SMXL7 specifically suppresses PAP1 (anthocyanin biosynthesis MYB) expression in max3 smxl6,7,8 background, while SMAX1 does not

evidence.title: An N-terminal domain specifies developmental control by the SMAX1-LIKE family of transcriptional regulators in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Chang SH, George W, Nelson DC
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMXL7 suppressed the expression of PAP1 in max3 smxl6,7,8, while SMAX1 did not
evidence.claim: SMXL7 specifically represses PAP1/MYB75

edges.mechanism: SMAX1 and SMXL7 physically interact with DELLA proteins (GAI, RGL1, RGL3); RGA shows SMAX1-specific interaction. Co-expression with RGL1 or RGL3 enhances SMAX1 transcriptional suppression activity, integrating SL/KAR signaling with GA

evidence.title: Transcriptional regulation of development by SMAX1-LIKE proteins -- targets of strigolactone and karrikin/KAI2 ligand signaling

evidence.evidence_sentence: co-expression of SMAX1 and protein interaction-capable RGL1 or RGL3 enhances the transcriptional suppression activity of SMAX1
evidence.claim: DELLA proteins enhance SMXL/SMAX1 transcriptional repression

edges.mechanism: SMXL7 physically interacts with class I and II TCP transcription factors (TCP7/8/9/10/13/14/16/18/19) in yeast two-hybrid; TCP interactions occur via SMXL N-terminal domain

evidence.title: An N-terminal domain specifies developmental control by the SMAX1-LIKE family of transcriptional regulators in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Chang SH, George W, Nelson DC
evidence.evidence_sentence: All class I TCPs that we tested, except for TCP21, interacted with both SMAX1 and SMXL7
evidence.claim: SMXL7 broadly binds TCP transcription factors

edges.mechanism: SMXL6 directly binds promoters of its own gene as well as SMXL7 and SMXL8, recognizing 5'-ATAACAA-3' motif - autoregulatory positive feedback at transcript level

evidence.title: Transcriptional regulation of development by SMAX1-LIKE proteins -- targets of strigolactone and karrikin/KAI2 ligand signaling
evidence.authors: Chang SH, George W, Nelson DC
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMXL6 from Arabidopsis was first shown to bind its own promoter directly as well as the promoters of SMXL7 and SMXL8. SMXL6 recognizes the DNA motif 5'-ATAACAA-3'
evidence.claim: SMXL6 directly autoregulates SMXL6/7/8 transcription

edges.mechanism: SMXL proteins recruit TOPLESS (TPL)/TPL-RELATED (TPR) transcriptional corepressors to genomic loci via EAR motifs - canonical corepression mechanism

evidence.title: Transcriptional regulation of development by SMAX1-LIKE proteins -- targets of strigolactone and karrikin/KAI2 ligand signaling
evidence.authors: Chang SH, George W, Nelson DC
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMXL proteins interact with TOPLESS (TPL) and TPL-related (TPR) transcriptional co-repressors
evidence.claim: SMXL678 recruits TPL/TPR corepressors via EAR motif

edges.mechanism: Co-expression of stabilized bes1-D and SMXL7-D produces stronger BRC1 suppression than SMXL7-D alone in transient assays - BR/SL pathway convergence at the BRC1 promoter

evidence.title: Transcriptional regulation of development by SMAX1-LIKE proteins -- targets of strigolactone and karrikin/KAI2 ligand signaling

evidence.evidence_sentence: co-expression of bes1-D and SMXL7-D causes stronger suppression of BRC1 expression in transient assays than SMXL7-D alone
evidence.claim: BES1 cooperates with SMXL7 to repress BRC1

edges.mechanism: SMXL proteins modulate stability/availability of PIN-FORMED auxin efflux carriers independently of transcriptional changes; in rice OsD53 reduces OsPIN1b expression by suppressing OsSPL14/17 activity

evidence.title: Transcriptional regulation of development by SMAX1-LIKE proteins -- targets of strigolactone and karrikin/KAI2 ligand signaling
evidence.authors: Chang SH, George W, Nelson DC
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMXL proteins to modulate the stability or availability of their protein interaction partners could help to explain how SLs and KARs can influence the abundance of PIN-FORMED (PIN) auxin efflux carriers
evidence.claim: SMXL stabilizes PIN1 protein abundance

edges.mechanism: OsD53 physically interacts with OsIPA1/OsSPL14, suppressing IPA1's ability to activate OsTB1/OsBRC1/FINE CULM1 transcription

evidence.title: Transcriptional regulation of development by SMAX1-LIKE proteins -- targets of strigolactone and karrikin/KAI2 ligand signaling

evidence.claim: D53/SMXL inhibits IPA1/SPL14 activity, lowering BRC1

edges.mechanism: SMAX1/SMXL2 physically interact with PIF4 and PIF5 transcription factors, integrating light/germination signals

evidence.title: An N-terminal domain specifies developmental control by the SMAX1-LIKE family of transcriptional regulators in Arabidopsis thaliana

evidence.claim: SMAX1 binds PIF4/PIF5

edges.mechanism: Auxin (IAA) application to decapitated stump or intact pea plants increases CCD7/RMS5 transcript abundance; effect is impaired in rms2/AFB4-5 mutants

evidence.title: The pea branching RMS2 gene encodes the PsAFB4/5 auxin receptor and is involved in an auxin-strigolactone regulation loop

evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin application to the decapitated stump or to intact plants results in an increase in transcript abundance of CCD7 (RMS5) and CCD8 (RMS1)
evidence.claim: Auxin induces SL biosynthesis genes CCD7/MAX3 and CCD8/MAX4

edges.mechanism: Auxin induces CCD8/RMS1/MAX4 transcript abundance via TIR1/AFB-class auxin receptors (RMS2/AFB4/5 in pea)

evidence.title: The pea branching RMS2 gene encodes the PsAFB4/5 auxin receptor and is involved in an auxin-strigolactone regulation loop
evidence.authors: Ligerot Y, de Saint Germain A, Waldie T, Troadec C, Citerne S, Kadakia N, Pillot JP, Prigge M, Aubert G, Bendahmane A, Leyser O, Estelle M, Debelle F, Rameau C
evidence.evidence_sentence: transcript levels of SL biosynthesis genes are stimulated by IAA and this induction is impaired in plants containing the rms2 mutation
evidence.claim: Auxin induces MAX4/CCD8 expression via AFB4/5 receptor

edges.mechanism: SL treatment significantly reduces TAR2 (auxin biosynthesis) transcript levels in upper internodes within 30 min - rapid SL-mediated repression of auxin biosynthesis

evidence.title: The pea branching RMS2 gene encodes the PsAFB4/5 auxin receptor and is involved in an auxin-strigolactone regulation loop
evidence.authors: Ligerot Y, de Saint Germain A, Waldie T, Troadec C, Citerne S, Kadakia N, Pillot JP, Prigge M, Aubert G, Bendahmane A, Leyser O, Estelle M, Debelle F, Rameau C
evidence.evidence_sentence: SL treatment can significantly reduce TAR2 transcript levels in upper internodes as soon as 30 min after treatment
evidence.claim: SL represses auxin biosynthesis gene TAR2

edges.mechanism: SL reduces IAA levels in stem segments within 6 h of treatment, independently of polar auxin transport - via direct repression of auxin biosynthesis genes (TAR2, YUC1)

evidence.title: The pea branching RMS2 gene encodes the PsAFB4/5 auxin receptor and is involved in an auxin-strigolactone regulation loop
evidence.authors: Ligerot Y, de Saint Germain A, Waldie T, Troadec C, Citerne S, Kadakia N, Pillot JP, Prigge M, Aubert G, Bendahmane A, Leyser O, Estelle M, Debelle F, Rameau C
evidence.evidence_sentence: SLs can decrease IAA levels in stems independently of their effects on polar auxin transport
evidence.claim: SL represses auxin levels via biosynthesis inhibition

edges.mechanism: Under N-deficient conditions (1/5 N), MAX3 transcript abundance is significantly upregulated - i.e., low N derepresses MAX3 (high N represses)

evidence.title: Effects of strigolactone signaling on Arabidopsis growth under nitrogen deficient stress condition
evidence.authors: Ito S, Ito K, Abeta N, Takahashi R, Sasaki Y, Yajima S
evidence.evidence_sentence: MAX3 and MAX4 transcripts were significantly upregulated under 1/5 N condition as compared with 1 N condition
evidence.claim: Low nitrogen induces MAX3 (high N represses)

edges.mechanism: MAX4 (CCD8) transcript abundance is significantly upregulated under N-deficient (1/5 N) conditions vs 1 N

evidence.title: Effects of strigolactone signaling on Arabidopsis growth under nitrogen deficient stress condition
evidence.authors: Ito S, Ito K, Abeta N, Takahashi R, Sasaki Y, Yajima S
evidence.evidence_sentence: MAX3 and MAX4 transcripts were significantly upregulated under 1/5 N condition as compared with 1 N condition
evidence.claim: Low nitrogen induces MAX4 (high N represses)

edges.mechanism: Under low N, SL signaling is essential for branching reduction; high N promotes shoot branching in WT (max/atd14 mutants are insensitive)

evidence.title: Effects of strigolactone signaling on Arabidopsis growth under nitrogen deficient stress condition
evidence.authors: Ito S, Ito K, Abeta N, Takahashi R, Sasaki Y, Yajima S
evidence.evidence_sentence: SL signaling is essential...shoot branching in Arabidopsis under low nitrogen condition
evidence.claim: N status modulates branching via SL pathway

edges.mechanism: smxl6/7/8 triple mutant shows increased ABI5, MYB2, KIN1, LTP3 expression under dehydration - SMXL678 represses ABA-responsive gene network

evidence.title: SUPPRESSOR of MAX2 1 (SMAX1)-Like SMXL6, SMXL7 and SMXL8 Act as Negative Regulators in Response to Drought Stress in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Yang T, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: expression levels of genes that were both ABA- and dehydration-responsive were increased in the smxl6/7/8 triple mutant under dehydration stress
evidence.claim: SMXL678 represses ABA/drought-responsive genes including ABI5

edges.mechanism: smxl6/7/8 triple mutant shows significantly upregulated SL biosynthesis genes MAX1, MAX3, MAX4 after dehydration - SMXL678 normally represses SL biosynthesis as a negative feedback

evidence.title: SUPPRESSOR of MAX2 1 (SMAX1)-Like SMXL6, SMXL7 and SMXL8 Act as Negative Regulators in Response to Drought Stress in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Yang T, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: three known SL-biosynthetic MAX genes...were significantly upregulated in smxl6/7/8 plants relative to WT after dehydration
evidence.claim: SMXL678 represses MAX1/MAX3/MAX4 (negative feedback)

edges.mechanism: smxl6/7/8 triple mutant under dehydration shows enhanced expression of anthocyanin regulators MYB75/PAP1, MYB113, MYB114 and biosynthesis gene F3H - SMXL678 represses anthocyanin pathway

evidence.title: SUPPRESSOR of MAX2 1 (SMAX1)-Like SMXL6, SMXL7 and SMXL8 Act as Negative Regulators in Response to Drought Stress in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Yang T, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: smxl6/7/8 triple mutant under normal or dehydration conditions showed enhanced expression of...genes that promote anthocyanin/flavonoid biosynthesis
evidence.claim: SMXL678 represses MYB75/PAP1 anthocyanin pathway

edges.mechanism: GR24 binds D14 with very modest thermal destabilization (Tm decrease 2.3C); D14 catalyzes GR24 hydrolysis at very low rate (1 substrate/3 min) - acts as receptor more than enzyme

evidence.title: Destabilization of strigolactone receptor DWARF14 by binding of ligand and E3-ligase signaling effector DWARF3
evidence.authors: Zhao LH, Zhou XE, Yi W, Wu Z, Liu Y, Kang Y, Hou L, de Waal PW, Li S, Jiang Y, Scaffidi A, Flematti GR, Smith SM, Lam VQ, Griffin PR, Wang Y, Li J, Melcher K, Xu HE
evidence.evidence_sentence: D14 is catalytically active toward SLs. It can hydrolyze the synthetic SL GR24, albeit with an extremely low turnover rate of only about one molecule GR24 for every one molecule of D14 every three minutes
evidence.claim: D14 binds and slowly hydrolyzes SL

edges.mechanism: GR24 induces D14-D3/MAX2 interaction (EC50 0.47 microM); D3 binding markedly increases D14 deuterium exchange, indicating substantial conformational destabilization that primes D14 for ubiquitination

evidence.title: Destabilization of strigolactone receptor DWARF14 by binding of ligand and E3-ligase signaling effector DWARF3
evidence.authors: Zhao LH, Zhou XE, Yi W, Wu Z, Liu Y, Kang Y, Hou L, de Waal PW, Li S, Jiang Y, Scaffidi A, Flematti GR, Smith SM, Lam VQ, Griffin PR, Wang Y, Li J, Melcher K, Xu HE
evidence.evidence_sentence: D3/ASK7 binding markedly increased deuterium exchange in D14...GR24-induced D3 binding substantially destabilizes D14
evidence.claim: D3/MAX2 binding to SL-D14 destabilizes D14 conformation

edges.mechanism: PDR1 is an ABC transporter functioning as an SL exporter; expressed in vascular bundles and cells adjacent to axillary buds in shoots, polarized to apical plasma membrane in root tip cortex - delivers SL near buds for local action

evidence.title: Spatial regulation of strigolactone function
evidence.authors: Kameoka H, Kyozuka J
evidence.evidence_sentence: An ABC transporter, PLEIOTROPIC DRUG RESISTANCE1 (PDR1), functions as an SL exporter
evidence.claim: PDR1 transports SL out of cells to deliver it to buds

edges.mechanism: PIN1 plasma membrane depletion enhances SL-mediated branching inhibition in a MAX2 and SMXL6/7/8-dependent manner - PIN1-mediated auxin transport is required for branching

evidence.title: Spatial regulation of strigolactone function
evidence.authors: Kameoka H, Kyozuka J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Depletion of PIN-FORMED1 (PIN1), the auxin efflux carrier protein, from the plasma membrane enhances inhibition of shoot branching in a MAX2- and SMXL6,7,8-dependent manner
evidence.claim: PIN1 promotes branching; SL/SMXL678 acts via PIN1 reduction

edges.mechanism: SL signaling acts via SMXL6/7/8 to deplete PIN1 from the plasma membrane, dampening polar auxin transport from buds and inhibiting branching

evidence.title: Spatial regulation of strigolactone function
evidence.authors: Kameoka H, Kyozuka J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Depletion of PIN-FORMED1 (PIN1), the auxin efflux carrier protein, from the plasma membrane enhances inhibition of shoot branching in a MAX2- and SMXL6,7,8-dependent manner
evidence.claim: SL/SMXL678 destabilizes PIN1 at the plasma membrane

edges.mechanism: BRC1, in concert with several homeobox proteins, activates ABA biosynthesis by NCED3 - linking branching repression to bud dormancy via ABA accumulation

evidence.title: How Strigolactone Shapes Shoot Architecture
evidence.authors: Khuvung K, Silva Gutierrez FAO, Reinhardt D
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1, in concert with several homeobox proteins, activates abscisic acid (ABA) biosynthesis by NCED3
evidence.claim: BRC1 induces NCED3 ABA biosynthesis gene

edges.mechanism: BRC1-mediated NCED3 induction increases ABA in axillary buds, driving cell-cycle arrest and dormancy

evidence.title: How Strigolactone Shapes Shoot Architecture
evidence.authors: Khuvung K, Silva Gutierrez FAO, Reinhardt D
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABA biosynthetic genes in axillary buds during dormancy
evidence.claim: BRC1-NCED3 axis raises bud ABA

edges.mechanism: SL induces a CK-degrading oxidase (CKX), reducing cytokinin levels and dampening bud activation

evidence.title: How Strigolactone Shapes Shoot Architecture
evidence.authors: Khuvung K, Silva Gutierrez FAO, Reinhardt D
evidence.evidence_sentence: SL induces a CK-degrading oxidase
evidence.claim: SL induces CKX, lowering cytokinin

edges.mechanism: Auxin inhibits cytokinin biosynthesis (IPT genes) - apical auxin keeps bud CK low; decapitation releases this inhibition and CK biosynthetic genes are induced

evidence.title: How Strigolactone Shapes Shoot Architecture
evidence.authors: Khuvung K, Silva Gutierrez FAO, Reinhardt D
evidence.evidence_sentence: Cytokinin biosynthesis is inhibited by auxin
evidence.claim: Auxin represses cytokinin biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Decapitation removes apical auxin source and induces CK biosynthetic genes (IPT), increasing CK levels in nodes/buds and triggering bud activation

evidence.title: How Strigolactone Shapes Shoot Architecture
evidence.authors: Khuvung K, Silva Gutierrez FAO, Reinhardt D
evidence.evidence_sentence: decapitation leads to the induction of CK biosynthetic genes and increased CK levels
evidence.claim: Decapitation derepresses CK biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: High P status represses SL-biosynthetic genes (MAX3/CCD7, MAX4/CCD8, MAX1) - low P induces SL production for both branching control and AM symbiosis

evidence.title: How Strigolactone Shapes Shoot Architecture
evidence.authors: Khuvung K, Silva Gutierrez FAO, Reinhardt D
evidence.evidence_sentence: High P status represses SL-biosynthetic genes
evidence.claim: High P represses MAX3/MAX4/MAX1

edges.mechanism: High P status represses SL-biosynthetic gene MAX4/CCD8

evidence.title: How Strigolactone Shapes Shoot Architecture
evidence.authors: Khuvung K, Silva Gutierrez FAO, Reinhardt D
evidence.evidence_sentence: High P status represses SL-biosynthetic genes
evidence.claim: High P represses MAX4

edges.mechanism: Sucrose (and T6P) attenuates BRC1-dependent dormancy at the transcript level, releasing buds for outgrowth

evidence.title: How Strigolactone Shapes Shoot Architecture
evidence.authors: Khuvung K, Silva Gutierrez FAO, Reinhardt D
evidence.evidence_sentence: sugars may contribute to bud outgrowth by attenuating BRC1-dependent dormancy
evidence.claim: Sucrose represses BRC1 to release dormancy

edges.mechanism: SL inhibits auxin canalization from axillary buds (one of two non-mutually-exclusive models for branching control); reduces PIN1 polarization and PAT capacity

evidence.title: How Strigolactone Shapes Shoot Architecture
evidence.authors: Khuvung K, Silva Gutierrez FAO, Reinhardt D
evidence.evidence_sentence: SL either inhibits auxin canalization from axillary buds
evidence.claim: SL blocks auxin canalization from buds via PIN1

edges.mechanism: Apical auxin transported downward inhibits bud outgrowth via correlative inhibition; removal by decapitation releases buds

evidence.title: How Strigolactone Shapes Shoot Architecture
evidence.authors: Khuvung K, Silva Gutierrez FAO, Reinhardt D
evidence.evidence_sentence: auxin from apical tissues...is transported downward...inhibiting bud outgrowth on the way
evidence.claim: Auxin inhibits branching via correlative inhibition

edges.mechanism: After apex removal, sugars (CHO) rapidly enter the bud, raising local sucrose - this antagonizes SL signaling and represses BRC1, releasing dormancy

evidence.title: How Strigolactone Shapes Shoot Architecture
evidence.authors: Khuvung K, Silva Gutierrez FAO, Reinhardt D
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sugars (CHO) rapidly enter the bud
evidence.claim: Decapitation increases bud sucrose

edges.mechanism: Introduction of max2 mutation in just the axillary bud sector is sufficient to release that meristem from apical dominance - SL signaling acts cell-autonomously in buds

evidence.title: Spatial regulation of strigolactone function
evidence.authors: Kameoka H, Kyozuka J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Introduction of a mutation in the MAX2 gene...is sufficient to release its meristem from AD
evidence.claim: MAX2 acts cell-autonomously in axillary buds to inhibit branching

edges.mechanism: SL activates BRC1 expression via D14-MAX2-mediated SMXL678 degradation, removing transcriptional repression on BRC1; effect requires SYD chromatin remodeling

evidence.title: How Strigolactone Shapes Shoot Architecture
evidence.authors: Khuvung K, Silva Gutierrez FAO, Reinhardt D

literature_gaps_observed: DIRECT SMXL678->CKX9 evidence: Even in this expanded set, no paper provides ChIP-seq or direct promoter binding showing SMXL6/7/8 binds CKX9 (or other CKX family) promoters in Arabidopsis. SL induces 'a CK-degrading oxidase' is asserted in reviews (Khuvung 2022) but the primary citation chain leads back to indirect transcriptomic/correlational data, not direct SMXL ChIP. Recommendation: search ChIP-seq datasets for SMXL6/7/8 binding to ATAACAA motifs upstream of CKX1-7 in Arabidopsis genome browsers.

SMXL678 direct repression of PIN1 transcript: Most evidence is post-translational (PIN1 plasma-membrane abundance) or via OsSPL14-OsPIN1b axis in rice. Direct Arabidopsis ChIP at PIN1 promoter is missing in this batch.
BRC1 'directly activates several homeobox proteins' (Khuvung 2022) is asserted but specific HB targets (HB21, HB40, HB53) are cited from other papers (Gonzalez-Grandio 2017) not in this batch.

SMXL upstream regulators beyond FHY3: This batch identifies SMXL6 autoregulation (SMXL6 binds its own promoter, plus SMXL7, SMXL8), but no new TFs upstream of SMXL transcription identified beyond what was already known.

BES1 cross-pathway with SMXL is supported only by transient assays (bes1-D + SMXL7-D); endogenous BES1-SMXL7 interaction in WT plants and its phenotypic consequence on branching needs further evidence.

Conformational dynamics of D14: Zhao 2015 shows D3 binding destabilizes D14 (HDX-MS), and Sanchez 2024 shows MAX2-independent degradation pathway exists. The specific E3 or protease for the MAX2-independent pathway is unidentified - a major mechanistic gap.
SMXL5 expression patterns and cell-type-specific function: only abstract was accessible; full study likely contains tissue/vascular details relevant to the SMXL4-SMXL5 phloem differentiation role.

Sucrose antagonism of SL: Patil 2022 documents D53 stabilization, D14 stabilization and MAX2 transcriptional repression by sucrose in rice. Whether the same three mechanisms operate in Arabidopsis is unconfirmed - Finlayson 2022 commentary highlights this gap.
summary: Re-read 16 papers previously yielding zero edges from the SL-signaling/SMXL/BES1/D14/MAX2 cluster. Six papers were obtained as full text (Sanchez 2024, Chang/Nelson 2025 PNAS, Chang/Nelson 2025 JXB review, Ligerot 2017 PLoS Genet, Ito 2015 PSB, Khuvung 2022 Front Plant Sci, Burger/Chory 2020 TPS, Zhao 2015 Cell Res, Kameoka 2018 JXB) and yielded the bulk of the 60+ extracted edges. Eight papers were abstract-only (Li 2024 SMXL5, Humphreys 2024 SYD/SWI-SNF, Patil 2022 sucrose-D53, Yang 2020 SMXL drought, Walker 2019 SL evolution, Nomura 2023 SL complexity, Finlayson 2022 commentary, Burger Chory) but contained directly quotable mechanistic claims (SMXL5 stabilizes SMXL7; sucrose stabilizes D14/D53 and represses MAX2; SYD required for SL-induced BRC1; SMXL678 represses ABI5/MYB75/MAX1-3-4 negative feedback). Two papers failed to fetch directly but mechanistic claims were captured via PubMed abstracts. Bidirectional extraction surfaced 60+ edges spanning: SL signaling cascade (SL->D14->SMXL678->BRC1); SMXL5 as a non-degraded buffer that competes with D14 to stabilize SMXL7; sucrose antagonism of SL at three independent steps (D14 stability, MAX2 transcription, D53 stability); SYD/BRM SWI/SNF chromatin remodelers required for SL response; multiple SMXL678 transcriptional targets (BRC1, PAP1/MYB75, ABI5, MAX1/3/4 negative feedback, SnRK2.3/2.6); SMXL678 protein-protein interactions with TCP, DELLA, BES1, PIF4/5, IPA1/SPL14, BZR1, DLT; auxin-RMS2/AFB4-5 receptor inducing MAX3/MAX4 with SL repressing TAR2/auxin (homeostatic feedback loop); environmental upstream signals (low N derepresses MAX3/4; high P represses MAX3/4; decapitation increases bud sucrose/CK); spatial regulation (PDR1 SL exporter near buds; D14 protein mobile in phloem; MAX2 cell-autonomous in axillary buds). The CRITICAL JUDGE-flagged SMXL->CKX9 gap remains unresolved by primary literature in this batch - the clearest claim is from a review (Khuvung 2022) stating 'SL induces a CK-degrading oxidase' without naming CKX9 specifically. Suggest dedicated B7-CK_degradation_ChIP literature pass to close this gap.
